decks.notes: The main strategy of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly swarm the board with aggressive creatures, like Guide of Souls and Ocelot Pride, while using burn spells like Galvanic Discharge to control the board and push through damage. With cards like Goblin Bombardment and Voice of Victory to provide additional reach, and strong threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer to close out games, this deck aims to apply constant pressure and overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. The inclusion of disruption spells like Mana Tithe and Static Prison helps to protect the deck's aggressive game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant) to generate a significant amount of mana. This allows the player to cast powerful spells and threats such as Karn, the Great Creator, Ugin, the Eye of the Storms, and Sire of Seven Deaths. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining control over the game with removal spells like Dismember and Kozilek's Command.

The deck focuses on leveraging powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones by quickly getting them into play from the graveyard using cards like Unearth and Persist. It also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Spell Pierce to control the opponent's game plan. The deck uses a reanimation strategy to cheat big creatures into play early and overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes a combination of card draw, removal, and graveyard manipulation to execute its game plan efficiently and consistently.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. It aims to disrupt the opponent's plans while slowly building up its own resources and eventually overwhelming the opponent with powerful spells like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Supreme Verdict. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion provides additional value and synergizes well with the deck's control strategy. The mana base is carefully crafted to support the deck's color requirements and maximize consistency in casting spells.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively play creatures with high power and utilize Leyline cards and Leyline of the Guildpact to deal heavy damage quickly. The deck also includes spells like Lightning Bolt and Tribal Flames to remove opponent's creatures and finish the game. The mana base is designed to support the multicolor creatures and spells, while Stubborn Denial provides protection against removal spells. Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Scion of Draco are key threats in this aggressive 4/5c Aggro deck.

The Boros Aggro deck focuses on a fast and aggressive playstyle, utilizing creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck also includes powerful support cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory to boost the attacking power of its creatures. To control the board, cards like Goblin Bombardment and Static Prison are included. With a mix of quick creatures and support cards, the Boros Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early in the game and secure a quick victory.

The main theme of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive creatures like Psychic Frog and Tasigur, the Golden Fang. The deck aims to out-value the opponent by making efficient trades and disrupting their game plan, eventually grinding them out of resources. It also utilizes powerful cards like Force of Negation and Thoughtseize to maintain control of the game. The deck's win condition is to chip away at the opponent's life total while maintaining control until they can no longer keep up.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with cards through discard effects and cycling, allowing for the fast and efficient deployment of powerful threats like Hollow One and Detective's Phoenix. Utilizing the delirium mechanic with Nethergoyf and Marauding Mako, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The inclusion of Faithless Looting and Burning Inquiry provides card advantage while also fueling the graveyard for synergies. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of speed and versatility.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to quickly deploy a wide range of powerful creatures and enchantments onto the battlefield, utilizing cards like Birthing Ritual and Ephemerate to accelerate the deck's tempo. By leveraging creatures with abilities like Eternal Witness and Guide of Souls, as well as lockdown options such as Skyclave Apparition and Ranger-Captain of Eos, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of aggro pressure and disruptive elements. The deck also includes some ramp and land fixing elements to ensure consistency in mana production.

This deck focuses on quickly playing artifacts, utilizing cards like Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble to ramp up and swarm the opponent with creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary. Alongside powerful artifacts like Engineered Explosives and Urza's Saga, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficient aggro plays. The inclusion of cards like Welding Jar and Claws of Gix provide protection and utility, while cards like Metallic Rebuke and Arcbound Ravager help control the battlefield and push for a fast victory. The overall strategy is to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent with an aggressive affinity-based gameplay.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful board presence quickly through aggressive creatures like Wild Nacatl, Ragavan, and Scion of Draco. The deck also includes efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Leyline Binding to clear the way for your creatures to attack. With access to multiple colors of mana, the deck can also use cards like Tribal Flames to close out the game quickly. Additionally, the deck features cards like Umezawa's Jitte and Stubborn Denial to help protect your creatures and disrupt your opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands to generate a large amount of mana quickly, allowing you to cast powerful spells and creatures such as Ugin, Karn, and Eldrazi creatures like Thought-Knot Seer. It aims to control the game by utilizing removal spells like Kozilek's Command and Dismember to deal with threats, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Glaring Fleshraker and Vexing Bauble. The deck can also use Karn, the Great Creator to shut down artifacts and access a sideboard with powerful hate cards.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by countering opponent's spells, removing threats, and manipulating the board state. It has a strong emphasis on card advantage and card selection, utilizing cards like Jace, the Mind Sculptor, Teferi, Time Raveler, and Telling Time. The deck also includes powerful control elements such as Counterspell, Solitude, and Terminus to deal with any threats that may arise. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Prismatic Ending and March of Otherworldly Light for additional removal options and flexibility in handling different types of threats.

The main focus of this deck is to deal damage quickly and efficiently to the opponent. By using aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear, Goblin Guide, and Eidolon of the Great Revel, combined with burn spells like Lightning Bolt, Lava Spike, and Skullcrack, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent from the very beginning of the game. The inclusion of cards like Boros Charm and Searing Blaze help to maintain that pressure and finish the opponent off before they have a chance to stabilize. The mana base is carefully crafted to support the aggressive nature of the deck.

This deck focuses on utilizing The Underworld Cookbook to fuel aggressive plays and card advantage. By sacrificing artifacts and creatures to the Cookbook, you can generate card advantage and set up powerful plays with cards like Persist and Ovalchase Daredevil. The deck also includes strong aggro cards like Archon of Cruelty, Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, and Lightning Bolt to close out games quickly. The synergy between sacrificing cards to the Cookbook and utilizing powerful creatures and spells makes this deck a formidable force in Modern 1 vs 1 matchups.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bringing them back onto the battlefield using spells like Unearth and Persist. It combines the strategy of graveyard recursion with disruption through cards like Thoughtseize and Spell Pierce. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a steady stream of threats that are difficult to deal with, leading to a fast and decisive victory. The deck aims to outpace the opponent and disrupt their game plan while setting up its own powerful combos.
